listings.title: 1971 Toyota Land Cruiser
listings.description: 1971 Toyota Land Cruiser FJ40 Ã¢?" Trail-Ready 4x4 with F-Series Inline-6 and Warn Winch Why This Car Is Special The 1971 Toyota Land Cruiser FJ40 occupies a singular place in the history of four-wheel drive vehicles. While American buyers were familiar with the Jeep CJ and International Scout, the FJ40 offered something different: a body-on-frame truck built to handle sustained abuse in remote terrain without the need for constant mechanical attention. Toyota had been refining the Land Cruiser formula since the early 1950s, developing it initially for military and commercial use in rugged markets like Australia, the Middle East, and South America. By 1971, the FJ40 had earned a reputation that no amount of advertising could manufacture Ã¢?" it was simply the truck that came back when others didn't.

listings.title: 2007 Honda S2000 2dr Conv
listings.description: If sunshine had a six-speed, it might look a lot like this yellow 2007 Honda S2000. Drop the power soft top, glance over the rear glass window, and youre ready for the kind of drive that reminds you why weekend plans dont always need a destinationjust a good road and a great convertible.Up front is a high-revving 2.2L DOHC PGM-FI 16-valve VTEC inline-4 running on gasoline, paired with a crisp 6-speed manual with overdrive. The drive-by-wire throttle makes responses feel immediate, while rear-wheel drive and a torque-sensing limited-slip differential help put power down with confidence when the road starts to twist. Add in independent double-wishbone suspension at all four corners, stabilizer bars, and power rack pinion steering, and youve got a car that feels eager, balanced, and communicativeexactly what an S2000 should be.

listings.title: 1994 Nissan 300ZX
listings.description: 1994 Nissan 300ZX Nissan was a relatively small automaker when it entered the international market in the 1960's and partnered with Yamaha to design a new sports car prototype to update the Nissan Fairlady. Nissan executives saw the prototype as a halo car that would improve their company's image in the minds of consumers. By 1964, Nissan realized that Yamaha's DOHC 2.0-liter engine was not meeting Nissan's expectations and the project was scrapped. Enter the Datsun 240Z, and the rest is history. For consignment, an original 1994 Nissan 300ZX, with a powerful 3.0 Liter V6 engine and a leather interior. In very good condition, well cared for.

listings.description: 1978 Toyota Land Cruiser FJ40 Ã¢?" Restored Body, Undercarriage, and Drivetrain in Cream over Black Vinyl Why This Car Is Special The 1978 Toyota Land Cruiser FJ40 sits at the center of one of the most devoted followings in the collector truck world, and for good reason. The FJ40 was produced from 1960 through 1984 in this body style, and the late-1970s examples like this one represent a sweet spot in the model's evolution. By 1978, Toyota had refined the FJ40's fit and finish considerably compared to early examples, while the truck still carried its original body-on-frame construction, solid front and rear axles, and mechanical simplicity that made the Land Cruiser a global standard for serious off-road capability.
